steps.title: Use the correlation gap
steps.content: The graph gives different correlations for participant P1: the model tracked P1's impressionist ratings more closely than P1's color-field ratings. If P1's actual ratings for the two styles were equal, different correlations imply the model did not predict equal ratings.

steps.title: Reject enjoyment claims
steps.content: A and B infer which style P1 enjoyed more, but correlations measure model fit, not preference. D says the predicted ratings were equal, which would not explain the different correlations. The answer is C.
